meta.description: Country citizenship routes compiled from a canonical D1 release plus the read-only legacy remainder.

acquisition_modes.ancestry: Citizenship through a parent, grandparent, wider descent rule, restoration, or documented heritage connection.
acquisition_modes.naturalization: Citizenship after residence or another qualifying domestic status.
acquisition_modes.birth: Citizenship or an accelerated route triggered by place of birth.
acquisition_modes.investment: Direct investor citizenship; residence-by-investment is not classified as citizenship by investment.

coverage_states.reviewed: The recorded finding has been checked against an official source.
coverage_states.partial: At least one rule is recorded, but this mode is not yet exhaustive.
coverage_states.pending: A credible legal basis exists but current operation is not verified.
coverage_states.unchecked: No route-level review has been completed for this mode.

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through an Andorran parent
routes.summary: Birth to an Andorran parent creates Andorran nationality. Birth in Andorra alone to two foreign parents is not general jus soli.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through an Andorran parent

routes.title: Andorran nationality through an Andorran parent
routes.summary: A child of an Andorran parent is Andorran under nationality law, subject to registration rules for birth abroad.

pathways.label: Andorran nationality through an Andorran parent

routes.title: Naturalization after long residence
routes.summary: Ordinary naturalization generally requires about twenty years permanent residence (ten if educated in Andorra), integration, and renunciation of prior nationality. Grant is discretionary.

pathways.label: Naturalization after long residence

routes.title: Citizenship by Investment Programme
routes.summary: Government-administered direct citizenship programme with contribution, real-estate and other approved investment options.

pathways.label: Citizenship by Investment Programme
pathways.note: The National Development Fund option currently starts at US$230,000. Other approved routes and fees differ, and the principal applicant must spend at least five days in Antigua and Barbuda during the first five calendar years.

sources.title: Antigua and Barbuda Citizenship by Investment Unit

routes.title: Citizenship by birth in Antigua and Barbuda
routes.summary: A person born in Antigua and Barbuda acquires citizenship at birth, subject to the Constitution’s diplomatic-immunity and hostile-occupation exceptions.

pathways.label: Citizenship by birth in Antigua and Barbuda

routes.title: Citizenship through a citizen parent
routes.summary: A person born abroad acquires citizenship at birth through a qualifying Antigua and Barbuda citizen parent under section 113 of the Constitution.

pathways.label: Citizenship through a citizen parent

routes.title: Naturalization after a qualifying residence period
routes.summary: The Citizenship Act requires residence throughout the twelve months immediately before application and at least five aggregate years during the preceding seven-year period.

pathways.label: Naturalization after a qualifying residence period
pathways.note: The eight-year window contains a continuous final year plus five aggregate years in the preceding seven; it is not an eight-year continuous-residence rule.

sources.title: Antigua and Barbuda Immigration — Citizenship FAQs

routes.title: Argentine citizenship by territorial birth
routes.summary: A person born in Argentine territory is generally an Argentine citizen at birth, subject to the statutory exception for children of foreign ministers and diplomatic-legation members residing in Argentina.

pathways.label: Argentine citizenship by territorial birth

sources.title: Argentina — current text of Citizenship Law No. 346

routes.title: Argentine citizenship through a native Argentine parent
routes.summary: A person born abroad to a native Argentine parent may opt for Argentine citizenship under Citizenship Law No. 346.

pathways.label: Argentine citizenship through a native Argentine parent

sources.title: Argentina — current text of Citizenship Law No. 346

routes.title: Naturalization after two years of continuous legal residence
routes.summary: Article 2(1) of Citizenship Law No. 346 permits an adult foreign national to request citizenship after two years of continuous legal residence immediately before applying.

pathways.label: Naturalization after two years of continuous legal residence

sources.title: Argentina — current text of Citizenship Law No. 346

routes.title: Citizenship following a relevant investment
routes.summary: The current law permits a foreign national to request citizenship, regardless of residence duration, after making an investment that qualifies as relevant under criteria established by the Ministry of Economy.

pathways.label: Relevant investment route
pathways.note: Decree 524/2025 provides a review process but no verified investment threshold or reliable public operating timeline.

sources.title: Argentina — current text of Citizenship Law No. 346
Argentina — Decree 524/2025 on citizenship by investment

routes.title: Citizenship by birth in Australia
routes.summary: A child born in Australia is a citizen when a parent is a citizen or permanent resident, or when the child is ordinarily resident in Australia for the first ten years of life.

pathways.label: Citizen or permanent-resident parent at birth
First ten years ordinarily resident in Australia

routes.title: Citizenship by conferral after residence
routes.summary: The general residence requirement is four years on a valid visa, including the final 12 months as a permanent resident or eligible Special Category Visa holder.

pathways.label: General residence route
pathways.note: Absence, character, test, and pledge requirements also apply.

sources.title: Australian Home Affairs — Citizenship residence calculator

routes.title: Citizenship by descent
routes.summary: A person born outside Australia may apply by descent when a parent was an Australian citizen at the time of birth.

pathways.label: Australian citizen parent at birth
pathways.note: If the parent was themselves a citizen by descent, a two-year lawful-presence condition can apply.

sources.title: Australian Home Affairs — Citizenship by descent

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through an Austrian parent
routes.summary: Birth to an Austrian parent creates Austrian citizenship. Birth in Austria alone to two foreign parents is not general jus soli.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through an Austrian parent

routes.title: Austrian citizenship through an Austrian parent
routes.summary: A child of an Austrian citizen parent is Austrian under the Citizenship Act. Separate declaration routes exist for certain descendants of Nazi-era victims.

pathways.label: Austrian citizenship through an Austrian parent

routes.title: Naturalization after ten years residence
routes.summary: Ordinary naturalization generally needs ten years continuous legal residence (including at least five with a settlement permit), German language, civic knowledge, livelihood, and usually renunciation of prior nationality. Shorter paths exist for EEA citizens and other statutory categories. Grant is discretionary.

pathways.label: Naturalization after ten years residence

routes.title: Citizenship through a qualifying Bahamian parent
routes.summary: The Constitution provides citizenship at birth or a registration route for a person born abroad through a qualifying Bahamian parent; the applicable rule depends on parentage and the circumstances of birth.

pathways.label: Citizenship through a qualifying Bahamian parent

routes.title: Citizenship connected to birth in The Bahamas
routes.summary: Birth in The Bahamas confers citizenship at birth when a parent is Bahamian. A person born there to non-citizen parents may instead apply for registration at age 18 and before turning 19, subject to the constitutional conditions.

pathways.label: Citizenship connected to birth in The Bahamas
pathways.note: The age-18 route is later registration connected to birthplace, not citizenship at birth.

routes.title: Naturalization after a qualifying residence period
routes.summary: The Nationality Act requires twelve months of residence immediately before application and at least six aggregate years during the preceding nine years, alongside character, English, civic-knowledge and permanent-home requirements.

pathways.label: Naturalization after a qualifying residence period
pathways.note: The ten-year statutory window contains a final continuous year plus six aggregate years in the preceding nine; it is not a ten-year continuous-residence rule.

routes.title: Citizenship by birth in Barbados
routes.summary: A person born in Barbados after 29 November 1966 is generally a citizen from birth, subject to narrow diplomatic-immunity and hostile-occupation exceptions.

pathways.label: Citizenship by birth in Barbados

routes.title: Citizenship through a qualifying Barbadian parent
routes.summary: A person born abroad acquires citizenship at birth through the constitutional parentage rules, including where at least one parent is a Barbadian citizen born in Barbados.

pathways.label: Citizenship through a qualifying Barbadian parent

routes.title: Naturalization after qualifying residence
routes.summary: An alien may apply after twelve continuous months immediately before the application and at least five aggregate years of residence during the preceding seven years, subject to character and residence-intention requirements.

pathways.label: Naturalization after qualifying residence
pathways.note: The Act uses a final continuous year plus five aggregate years in the preceding seven. It is neither six continuous years nor eight continuous years.

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through a Belgian parent
routes.summary: Birth to a Belgian parent creates Belgian nationality. Birth in Belgium alone to two foreign parents is not unrestricted jus soli.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through a Belgian parent

routes.title: Belgian nationality through a Belgian parent
routes.summary: A child of a Belgian parent is Belgian under the nationality code, subject to registration rules for birth abroad.

pathways.label: Belgian nationality through a Belgian parent

routes.title: Nationality declaration after five years residence
routes.summary: Adults with five years legal residence may apply for Belgian nationality by declaration if they meet integration, language, and economic participation conditions. Naturalization by parliament remains a separate exceptional track. Not automatic after five years.

pathways.label: Nationality declaration after five years residence

routes.title: Citizenship by birth in Bolivia or through a Bolivian parent
routes.summary: Persons born in Bolivian territory are Bolivian by birth (with the constitutional foreign-service exceptions). Parentage rules also transmit Bolivian nationality.

pathways.label: Citizenship by birth in Bolivia or through a Bolivian parent

routes.title: Bolivian nationality through a Bolivian parent
routes.summary: Children of a Bolivian parent are Bolivian by birth under the Constitution, including birth abroad subject to the statutory rules.

pathways.label: Bolivian nationality through a Bolivian parent

routes.title: Naturalization after continuous residence
routes.summary: Foreigners may naturalize after more than three years uninterrupted legal residence, or two years with a Bolivian spouse, child, or foster parent, subject to legal status and statutory process.

pathways.label: Naturalization after continuous residence

routes.title: Brazilian citizenship by territorial birth
routes.summary: A person born in Brazil is generally Brazilian at birth, including a child of foreign parents, unless the parents are serving their country.

pathways.label: Brazilian citizenship by territorial birth

routes.title: Brazilian citizenship through a Brazilian parent
routes.summary: A child born abroad to a Brazilian parent can acquire Brazilian nationality through the constitutional service, consular-registration, or later residence-and-option rules.

pathways.label: Brazilian citizenship through a Brazilian parent

routes.title: Brazilian naturalization after residence
routes.summary: Ordinary naturalization generally requires four years of residence. The minimum falls to one year for a parent of a Brazilian child and for a person originating from a Portuguese-speaking country, subject to the remaining conditions.

pathways.label: Ordinary four-year residence route
pathways.note: Capacity, Portuguese-language ability and absence of a disqualifying criminal conviction also apply.

pathways.label: One-year route for an origin country with Portuguese as an official language
pathways.note: Article 12 also requires good moral character.

pathways.label: One-year route for a parent of a Brazilian child
pathways.note: The child is Brazilian at birth. A parent can seek family-reunification residence, and having a Brazilian child reduces the naturalization residence minimum to one year; citizenship is not automatic. Grandparents may qualify for family-reunification residence as second-degree ascendants, but receive no automatic citizenship or child-based one-year naturalization shortcut.

sources.title: Brazil Ministry of Justice — naturalization residence periods

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through a citizen parent
routes.summary: Birth to a Solomon Islands citizen parent creates citizenship. Birth in the Solomon Islands alone to two foreign parents is not general jus soli.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through a citizen parent

routes.title: Solomon Islands citizenship through a citizen parent
routes.summary: A child of a Solomon Islands citizen parent is a citizen under nationality law, subject to registration rules.

pathways.label: Solomon Islands citizenship through a citizen parent

routes.title: Naturalization after five years residence
routes.summary: Adults generally need about five years residence (commonly framed as five of the preceding ten years), good character, and language or cultural integration. Dual nationality is now generally permitted. Grant is discretionary.

pathways.label: Naturalization after five years residence

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through a Bruneian parent
routes.summary: Birth to a Bruneian parent creates Bruneian nationality. Birth in Brunei alone to two foreign parents is not general jus soli.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through a Bruneian parent

routes.title: Bruneian nationality through a Bruneian parent
routes.summary: A child of a subject of His Majesty is Bruneian under the Nationality Act, subject to registration rules for birth abroad.

pathways.label: Bruneian nationality through a Bruneian parent

routes.title: Naturalization after twenty years residence
routes.summary: Adults generally need twenty years aggregate residence in the preceding twenty-five years, including the two years immediately before application, Malay language proficiency, good character, and intention to settle. Dual nationality is not recognized; prior nationality must cease. Grant is discretionary.

pathways.label: Naturalization after twenty years residence

routes.title: Naturalization for a person of Bulgarian origin
routes.summary: A person of Bulgarian origin may apply under article 15 without the ordinary residence, income, language and release-of-prior-citizenship conditions. The applicant must establish the family relationship with at least one ancestor of Bulgarian origin, up to the third degree inclusive, using the required official documents.

pathways.label: Naturalization for a person of Bulgarian origin

sources.title: Bulgarian Ministry of Justice — Citizenship Act

routes.title: Citizenship by birth where no other citizenship is acquired
routes.summary: A person born in Bulgaria is a citizen by place of birth if they do not acquire another citizenship by origin; a foundling with unknown parents is presumed born in Bulgaria.

pathways.label: Citizenship by birth where no other citizenship is acquired

sources.title: Bulgarian Ministry of Justice — Citizenship Act

routes.title: Former investor-citizenship provisions
routes.summary: Bulgaria repealed the special investment-based citizenship provisions in 2022. Investment or property ownership should not be presented as a current direct route to Bulgarian citizenship; any residence obtained on another legal basis follows the applicable ordinary nationality rules.

pathways.label: Former investor-citizenship provisions
pathways.note: Historical negative retained to prevent stale investor-citizenship recommendations.

sources.title: Bulgarian National Assembly — investment citizenship repealed
Bulgarian Ministry of Justice — Citizenship Act

routes.title: Ordinary naturalization after permanent residence
routes.summary: Bulgaria's ordinary route generally requires an adult to have held permanent or long-term residence for at least five years, alongside criminal-record, income or occupation, Bulgarian-language and—unless an exception applies—prior-citizenship release requirements. This is five years after the qualifying residence status, not a promise of citizenship after five years of any lawful stay.

pathways.label: Ordinary naturalization after permanent residence
pathways.note: The five years run from obtaining permanent or long-term residence, not merely from first arrival.

sources.title: Bulgarian Ministry of Justice — Citizenship Act
Bulgarian Ministry of Justice — Citizenship Directorate

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through a Cambodian parent
routes.summary: Birth to a Cambodian parent creates Cambodian nationality. Birth in Cambodia alone to two foreign parents is not general jus soli.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through a Cambodian parent

routes.title: Cambodian nationality through a Cambodian parent
routes.summary: A child of a Cambodian parent is Cambodian under nationality law.

pathways.label: Cambodian nationality through a Cambodian parent

sources.title: Cambodia Law on Nationality 1996 (CIB English text)

routes.title: Investor and donor naturalization exists in law; current operation needs verification
routes.summary: Cambodia's nationality law contains investment and donation provisions, but a current government-administered programme and operational requirements have not been verified for live recommendations.

pathways.label: Investor and donor naturalization exists in law; current operation needs verification

sources.title: Cambodia Law on Nationality 1996 (CIB English text)

routes.title: Naturalization after seven years residence
routes.summary: Foreign adults may apply after about seven years continuous legal residence, with language and character conditions. Grant is discretionary.

pathways.label: Naturalization after seven years residence

sources.title: Cambodia Law on Nationality 1996 (CIB English text)

routes.title: Citizenship by birth in Canada
routes.summary: A person born in Canada is generally a citizen at birth, with a narrow exception for children of certain foreign diplomatic personnel.

pathways.label: Born in Canada

sources.title: IRCC — Check if you may be a Canadian citizen

routes.title: Citizenship through a Canadian parent
routes.summary: A child born abroad to a Canadian parent is generally a citizen; from 15 December 2025, a Canadian parent also born abroad must usually have spent 1,095 days in Canada before the child’s birth.

pathways.label: Canadian parent born or naturalized in Canada
Canadian parent also born abroad
pathways.note: This substantial-connection test applies to births on or after 15 December 2025.

sources.title: IRCC — Check if you may be a Canadian citizen

routes.title: Citizenship grant after physical presence
routes.summary: An adult permanent resident generally needs 1,095 days of physical presence in the five-year eligibility period, including at least 730 days as a permanent resident.

pathways.label: Standard adult grant
pathways.note: Tax, language, test, oath, and prohibition rules can also apply.

routes.title: BOTC citizenship at birth through a qualifying parent
routes.summary: A child born in Cayman acquires BOTC at birth when a parent is a BOTC or is settled in the territory, subject to the British Nationality Act and date-specific rules.

pathways.label: BOTC citizenship at birth through a qualifying parent

routes.title: BOTC citizenship by descent through a qualifying parent
routes.summary: British Overseas Territories citizenship connected to Cayman can pass to a child born abroad under the British Nationality Act’s descent and registration rules.

pathways.label: BOTC citizenship by descent through a qualifying parent

routes.title: BOTC naturalization after qualifying residence
routes.summary: An adult can apply to naturalize as a BOTC connected to Cayman under the British Nationality Act after the statutory residence period; Caymanian status is a separate immigration status.

pathways.label: BOTC naturalization after qualifying residence
pathways.note: The general BOTC naturalization schedule uses five years. From 1 May 2026, Caymanian status based on naturalization generally requires 20 years lawful ordinary residence or 10 years after BOTC naturalization or registration.

sources.title: Cayman Islands Government — BOTC registration and naturalisation

routes.title: Nationality by birth in Chile
routes.summary: Persons born in Chilean territory are Chilean by birth, except children of transient foreigners or of foreigners who are in Chile in the service of their government. Statelessness safeguards and later option routes may apply to some excluded children.

pathways.label: Nationality by birth in Chile

routes.title: Nationality through a Chilean parent or grandparent
routes.summary: Children of a Chilean father or mother born abroad, and further generations covered by Article 10, may hold or claim Chilean nationality under the constitutional descent rules, including where a parent or grandparent acquired nationality by birth or naturalization as applicable.

pathways.label: Nationality through a Chilean parent or grandparent

sources.title: Chile SERMIG — Chilean citizenship / Carta de Nacionalización

routes.title: Carta de Nacionalización after permanent residence
routes.summary: Adult permanent residents may seek a letter of naturalization after five or more years of residence counted from the electronic stamp that led to permanent residence. A reduced two-year continuous residence path applies for specified family ties to Chileans, including a two-year marriage registered in Chile with shared household.

pathways.label: Carta de Nacionalización after permanent residence
pathways.note: Family-tie applicants may qualify after two continuous years from the electronic stamp. The grant remains discretionary presidential/administrative naturalization, not an automatic right after five years.

sources.title: Chile SERMIG — Chilean citizenship / Carta de Nacionalización

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through a Chinese parent
routes.summary: Birth to a Chinese parent generally creates Chinese nationality. The PRC does not recognize dual nationality for Chinese nationals.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through a Chinese parent

routes.title: Chinese nationality through a Chinese parent
routes.summary: A child of a Chinese national parent is Chinese under the Nationality Law, subject to rules for births abroad where the parent has settled abroad and acquired foreign nationality.

pathways.label: Chinese nationality through a Chinese parent

routes.title: Naturalization as a Chinese national
routes.summary: Foreign nationals or stateless persons may apply if they are near relatives of Chinese nationals, have settled in China, or have other legitimate reasons, and are willing to abide by the Constitution and laws. Approved naturalizers must not retain foreign nationality. Grant is discretionary and uncommon.

pathways.label: Naturalization as a Chinese national

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through a R.O.C. parent or foundling rule
routes.summary: A person born to a R.O.C. national parent is a R.O.C. national at birth. A person born in the territory whose parents cannot be ascertained or were both stateless is also a national. Birth in Taiwan alone to identified foreign parents is not general jus soli.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through a R.O.C. parent or foundling rule

sources.title: Taiwan (R.O.C.) Nationality Act — Laws & Regulations Database

routes.title: R.O.C. nationality through a R.O.C. parent
routes.summary: A person has R.O.C. nationality if the father or mother was a R.O.C. national at the time of birth, or if a parent died before the birth while a R.O.C. national. Restoration routes exist for persons who previously lost nationality under the Act.

pathways.label: R.O.C. nationality through a R.O.C. parent

sources.title: Taiwan (R.O.C.) Nationality Act — Laws & Regulations Database

routes.title: Naturalization after consecutive legal residence
routes.summary: A foreign national or stateless person domiciled in the R.O.C. may apply for naturalization after legally residing more than 183 days each year for at least five consecutive years, with capacity, good conduct, livelihood, and basic national-language and civic knowledge. Shorter three-year floors apply for spouses, certain family ties, persons born in the territory, and other Article 4 categories. Permission is granted by the Ministry of the Interior; eligibility is not an entitlement.

pathways.label: Naturalization after consecutive legal residence
pathways.note: Article 9 generally requires a certificate of loss of original nationality within one year of naturalization permission, with exemptions for high-level professionals, special contribution, and cases where loss is impossible for reasons not attributable to the applicant.

sources.title: Taiwan (R.O.C.) Nationality Act — Laws & Regulations Database

routes.title: Colombian nationality by conditional territorial birth
routes.summary: A child born in Colombia to foreign parents is Colombian by birth when at least one parent was domiciled in Colombia at the time of birth; a separate exception protects a child whom no state recognizes as a national.

pathways.label: Colombian nationality by conditional territorial birth

routes.title: Colombian nationality through a Colombian parent
routes.summary: A person born abroad to a Colombian mother or father is Colombian by birth after registering at a Colombian consulate or becoming domiciled in Colombia.

pathways.label: Colombian nationality through a Colombian parent

routes.title: Colombian naturalization after residence
routes.summary: Law 2332 generally requires five years of continuous domicile as a Resident Visa holder, reduced to two years for a qualifying Colombian spouse or permanent partner, Colombian child, or verified reciprocal treatment.

pathways.label: Ordinary five-year Resident Visa route
pathways.note: Naturalization remains a sovereign and discretionary decision.

pathways.label: Two-year family or reciprocity route
pathways.note: The reduction applies to a Colombian spouse or permanent partner, Colombian children, or verified reciprocal treatment by the origin state.

routes.title: Visitor student visa time does not accumulate toward permanent residence
routes.summary: Colombia's current student route is a Visitor (V) visa. The Foreign Ministry states that study time does not count toward a Resident (R) visa by accumulated time; article 90 lists qualifying Migrant (M) categories and does not include the V student visa.

pathways.label: V student residence-credit finding
pathways.note: The V student visa does not accumulate qualifying time toward a Resident Visa under the cited rules.

sources.title: Colombia Foreign Ministry — V Student Visa

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through a Croatian parent
routes.summary: Birth to a Croatian parent creates Croatian citizenship. Birth in Croatia alone to two foreign parents is not general jus soli.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through a Croatian parent

routes.title: Croatian citizenship through a Croatian parent
routes.summary: A child of a Croatian citizen parent is Croatian. Separate naturalization tracks exist for emigrant descendants and affiliation with the Croatian nation.

pathways.label: Croatian citizenship through a Croatian parent

routes.title: Naturalization after eight years residence
routes.summary: Ordinary residence naturalization generally needs eight years continuous registered residence with permanent residence status, Croatian language and culture knowledge, and usually renunciation of prior nationality. Separate tracks exist for marriage, emigrants, and national interest.

pathways.label: Naturalization after eight years residence

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through a Cypriot parent
routes.summary: A person born abroad after 16 August 1960 can use the consular-birth route where a mother or father was a Cypriot citizen at the time of birth, subject to statutory exceptions and registration.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through a Cypriot parent

sources.title: Cyprus Ministry of Interior — Citizenship due to Cypriot origin

routes.title: Cypriot citizenship due to origin
routes.summary: Cypriot-origin registration routes cover people born abroad and specified descendants, using the applicable M121, M123, M124 or M126 procedure.

pathways.label: Cypriot citizenship due to origin

sources.title: Cyprus Ministry of Interior — Citizenship due to Cypriot origin

routes.title: Cypriot naturalization by residence
routes.summary: The ordinary route requires twelve continuous months immediately before application and at least seven cumulative lawful years in the preceding ten years, plus language, civic, character and resources tests.

pathways.label: Cypriot naturalization by residence

sources.title: Cyprus Ministry of Interior — Naturalization due to residence

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through a Czech parent
routes.summary: Birth to a Czech parent creates Czech citizenship. Birth in Czechia alone to two foreign parents is not general jus soli.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through a Czech parent

routes.title: Czech citizenship through a Czech parent
routes.summary: A child of a Czech citizen parent is Czech under the Citizenship Act.

pathways.label: Czech citizenship through a Czech parent

routes.title: Naturalization after permanent residence
routes.summary: Adults generally need five years of permanent residence (or three for EU citizens and some family categories), Czech language and civics, livelihood, and good conduct. Dual nationality is generally allowed. Grant is discretionary.

pathways.label: Naturalization after permanent residence

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through a Danish parent
routes.summary: Birth to a Danish parent creates Danish citizenship. Birth in Denmark alone to two foreign parents is not general jus soli.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through a Danish parent

routes.title: Danish citizenship through a Danish parent
routes.summary: A child of a Danish citizen parent is Danish under the Nationality Act.

pathways.label: Danish citizenship through a Danish parent

routes.title: Naturalization after long residence
routes.summary: Adults generally need about nine years continuous residence, permanent residence, Danish language and civics tests, self-support, and good conduct. Parliament grants nationality by statute. Dual nationality is generally allowed.

pathways.label: Naturalization after long residence

routes.title: Dominica Citizenship by Investment Programme
routes.summary: Government-administered direct citizenship programme offering a public-fund contribution and approved real-estate route.

pathways.label: Dominica Citizenship by Investment Programme
pathways.note: The official minimum starts at US$200,000 for a single applicant under either the fund contribution or approved-real-estate route; fees and family thresholds vary.

routes.title: Citizenship by birth in Dominica
routes.summary: A person born in Dominica acquires citizenship at birth, subject to the constitutional exceptions for certain diplomatic and hostile-occupation circumstances.

pathways.label: Citizenship by birth in Dominica

sources.title: Constitution of the Commonwealth of Dominica

routes.title: Dominican citizenship through a citizen parent
routes.summary: The Constitution provides citizenship by descent for a person born outside Dominica to a qualifying Dominican parent.

pathways.label: Dominican citizenship through a citizen parent

sources.title: Constitution of the Commonwealth of Dominica

routes.title: Naturalization after seven years of residence
routes.summary: Government forms provide a citizenship application route after seven years of residence, subject to ministerial approval and the supporting-document requirements.

pathways.label: Naturalization after seven years of residence

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through an Ecuadorian parent or birth in Ecuador
routes.summary: Birth to an Ecuadorian parent, or birth in Ecuador under the Constitution, creates Ecuadorian nationality.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through an Ecuadorian parent or birth in Ecuador

routes.title: Ecuadorian nationality through an Ecuadorian parent
routes.summary: A person born to an Ecuadorian father or mother is Ecuadorian, including birth abroad subject to registration rules.

pathways.label: Ecuadorian nationality through an Ecuadorian parent

routes.title: Naturalization after legal residence
routes.summary: Foreign adults may seek naturalization after three years of continuous legal residence under the nationality and immigration framework, with shorter tracks for spouses and other statutory categories. Grant is discretionary.

pathways.label: Naturalization after legal residence

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through an Estonian parent
routes.summary: Birth to an Estonian parent creates Estonian citizenship. Birth in Estonia alone to two foreign parents is not general jus soli.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through an Estonian parent

routes.title: Estonian citizenship through an Estonian parent
routes.summary: A child of an Estonian citizen parent is Estonian under the Citizenship Act. Birth citizenship has different dual-nationality treatment than naturalization.

pathways.label: Estonian citizenship through an Estonian parent

routes.title: Naturalization after eight years residence
routes.summary: Adults generally need eight years residence on a permit or right of residence, Estonian language and civics knowledge, permanent residence, livelihood, and renunciation of prior nationality. Grant follows statutory conditions.

pathways.label: Naturalization after eight years residence

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through a Fijian parent
routes.summary: Birth to a Fijian parent creates Fijian citizenship under nationality law. Birth in Fiji alone to two foreign parents is not unrestricted jus soli.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through a Fijian parent

sources.title: Fiji Ministry of Immigration - citizenship by registration and naturalization

routes.title: Fijian citizenship through a Fijian parent
routes.summary: A child of a Fijian citizen parent may register as a citizen. Adult children of citizens use a registration track with residence conditions.

pathways.label: Fijian citizenship through a Fijian parent

sources.title: Fiji Ministry of Immigration - citizenship by registration and naturalization

routes.title: Naturalization after five of ten years lawful presence
routes.summary: Adults may naturalize after lawful presence in Fiji for a total of five of the ten years immediately before application (visitor and student time excluded). Dual nationality is generally allowed. Grant is discretionary.

pathways.label: Naturalization after five of ten years lawful presence

sources.title: Fiji Ministry of Immigration - citizenship by registration and naturalization
Fiji Ministry of Foreign Affairs - Fiji citizenship

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through a Finnish parent
routes.summary: Birth to a Finnish parent creates Finnish citizenship. Birth in Finland alone to two foreign parents is not general jus soli.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through a Finnish parent

routes.title: Finnish citizenship through a Finnish parent
routes.summary: A child of a Finnish citizen parent is Finnish under the Citizenship Act. Declaration routes also exist for former citizens and certain Nordic cases.

pathways.label: Finnish citizenship through a Finnish parent

routes.title: Citizenship after eight years residence
routes.summary: Adults generally need eight years legal residence in Finland. Five years can suffice with the required language skills, a Finnish spouse, or statelessness. Dual nationality is generally allowed. Grant is discretionary.

pathways.label: Citizenship after eight years residence

sources.title: Finnish Immigration Service - period of residence

routes.title: Citizenship through birth and residence in France
routes.summary: Birth in France alone is not generally enough. Limited parentage or statelessness cases confer citizenship at birth; otherwise a child of foreign parents can acquire it from age 13, 16, or automatically at 18 when the residence conditions are met.

pathways.label: Born in France to a parent also born in France
pathways.note: Citizenship from birth under the double-jus-soli rule, subject to the diplomatic exception.

pathways.label: Stateless parents or no nationality transmitted
pathways.note: Citizenship from birth where the child would otherwise receive no nationality from either parent.

pathways.label: Parent declaration from age 13
pathways.note: From age 13, a parent may declare with the child’s consent after at least five years of habitual residence in France since age 8.

pathways.label: Personal declaration from age 16
pathways.note: At age 16 or 17, the child may declare while living in France after at least five years of habitual residence since age 11.

pathways.label: Automatic acquisition at age 18
pathways.note: Automatic at age 18 if the person lives in France then and has accumulated at least five years of habitual residence since age 11.

sources.title: Service-Public — French nationality for a child born in France

routes.title: Citizenship through a French parent
routes.summary: A child is French when at least one parent is French.

pathways.label: At least one French parent
pathways.note: The entitlement is by filiation; documentation and registration time are separate.

routes.title: Naturalization after residence
routes.summary: French Civil Code article 21-17 ordinarily requires five years of habitual residence and does not exclude lawful student residence. Article 21-18 reduces that period to two years for a person who successfully completes two years of higher education toward a diploma from a French university or higher-education institution. A valid permit, continuing residence, assimilation, language and stable professional integration remain material; naturalization is discretionary.

facts.reduction_condition: Successfully complete two years of higher education toward a diploma from a French university or higher-education institution

pathways.label: Ordinary residence
pathways.note: Eligibility period only; naturalization remains discretionary.

pathways.label: French higher-education reduction
pathways.note: Reduced eligibility period, not a processing-time promise.

routes.title: Georgian citizenship through a Georgian parent
routes.summary: A person acquires Georgian citizenship by birth when at least one parent is a Georgian citizen.

pathways.label: Georgian citizenship through a Georgian parent

sources.title: Organic Law of Georgia on Georgian Citizenship
Georgia Public Service Development Agency — Citizenship

routes.title: Citizenship at birth under anti-statelessness safeguards
routes.summary: A child born in Georgia can acquire citizenship under narrow statutory safeguards, including where qualifying parents are stateless or the other parent is unknown.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth under anti-statelessness safeguards

sources.title: Organic Law of Georgia on Georgian Citizenship
Georgia Public Service Development Agency — Citizenship

routes.title: Ordinary naturalization after ten years
routes.summary: An adult may apply after ten consecutive years of lawful residence, subject to the Georgian language, history and law test and a qualifying economic connection.

pathways.label: Ordinary naturalization after ten years
pathways.note: A Georgian citizen’s spouse has a separate five-year simplified route. Eligibility does not guarantee the presidential grant.

sources.title: Organic Law of Georgia on Georgian Citizenship
Georgia Public Service Development Agency — Citizenship

routes.title: Citizenship by birth in Germany
routes.summary: A child of foreign parents born in Germany acquires citizenship when one parent meets the residence and permanent-status conditions.

pathways.label: Parent resident for five years with permanent status
pathways.note: The five-year period is a condition already met by the parent before birth.

routes.title: Citizenship through a German parent
routes.summary: A child generally acquires German citizenship at birth when at least one parent is German.

pathways.label: German parent at birth
pathways.note: Citizenship is acquired at birth when the statutory conditions are met.

routes.title: Naturalization after residence
routes.summary: The standard entitlement uses five years of lawful ordinary residence, with a shorter rule for spouses of Germans.

pathways.label: Standard five-year route
pathways.note: Other requirements in section 10 still apply.

pathways.label: Spouse or registered partner of a German
pathways.note: The residence and relationship requirements operate together.

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through a Ghanaian parent
routes.summary: Birth to a Ghanaian parent creates Ghanaian citizenship under the applicable constitutional date rules. Birth in Ghana alone to two foreign parents is not general jus soli.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through a Ghanaian parent

routes.title: Ghanaian citizenship through a Ghanaian parent
routes.summary: A child of a Ghanaian citizen parent is Ghanaian under the Citizenship Act and Constitution date rules.

pathways.label: Ghanaian citizenship through a Ghanaian parent

routes.title: Naturalization after five years residence
routes.summary: Applicants need twelve months residence immediately before applying and at least five years aggregate residence in the prior seven years, plus language, character, and integration conditions. Ministerial approval with presidential assent; discretionary.

pathways.label: Naturalization after five years residence

routes.title: Citizenship following birth and schooling in Greece
routes.summary: A child born in Greece to foreign parents can acquire citizenship by declaration where the statutory parent-residence, lawful-status and Greek-school conditions are met.

pathways.label: Citizenship following birth and schooling in Greece
pathways.note: This is a declaration route connected to birth and schooling, not unconditional jus soli.

sources.title: Greek Ministry of Interior — Citizenship Code

routes.title: Citizenship routes through a Greek parent
routes.summary: Greek nationality law provides acquisition and declaration procedures for people born to a Greek parent, with separate documentary routes depending on birth date, registration and the parents' marital or acknowledgment status. This record confirms the route family but does not collapse those distinct procedures into one universal checklist.

pathways.label: Citizenship routes through a Greek parent

sources.title: Greek Ministry of Interior — Citizenship supporting documents

routes.title: Ordinary naturalization after residence
routes.summary: Greece's ordinary naturalization route for non-co-ethnic foreign nationals generally uses seven years of legal residence, with distinct eligible residence categories and supporting documents. Applicants must also satisfy the applicable integration, language, civic-knowledge and character requirements; residence eligibility is not an automatic grant.

pathways.label: Ordinary naturalization after residence

sources.title: Greek Ministry of Interior — Citizenship supporting documents
Greek Ministry of Interior — seven-year naturalization period

routes.title: Citizenship by Investment Programme
routes.summary: Government-administered direct citizenship programme with National Transformation Fund and approved-project routes.

pathways.label: Citizenship by Investment Programme
pathways.note: The official programme page confirms the contribution and approved-project routes. Monetary thresholds are intentionally omitted here because the same page currently displays an older minimum and should not be treated as a reliable current fee schedule.

routes.title: Citizenship by birth in Grenada
routes.summary: A person born in Grenada acquires citizenship at birth, subject to the constitutional diplomatic-immunity and hostile-occupation exceptions.

pathways.label: Citizenship by birth in Grenada

routes.title: Citizenship through a citizen parent
routes.summary: A person born outside Grenada acquires citizenship at birth when a qualifying parent is a Grenadian citizen.

pathways.label: Citizenship through a citizen parent

routes.title: Naturalization after a seven-year residence history
routes.summary: An alien may apply for discretionary naturalization using the official form’s seven-year residence or government-service history, with character, solvency and intention-to-reside review.

pathways.label: Naturalization after a seven-year residence history
pathways.note: The official application records the seven years immediately before filing. It does not establish an automatic grant after seven continuous years.

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through a Hungarian parent
routes.summary: A child acquires Hungarian citizenship at birth when at least one parent is a Hungarian citizen, regardless of the place of birth. Birth in Hungary alone to foreign parents is not general jus soli.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through a Hungarian parent

sources.title: Hungary Government — acquisition of Hungarian nationality

routes.title: Citizenship through a Hungarian parent or simplified origin naturalization
routes.summary: A person is a Hungarian citizen at birth if a parent is a Hungarian citizen. Separately, simplified naturalization is available without a multi-year residence period for applicants who prove a Hungarian ancestor or Hungarian origin and demonstrate Hungarian language knowledge, or who meet the long marriage-to-Hungarian criteria.

pathways.label: Citizenship through a Hungarian parent or simplified origin naturalization
pathways.note: Parent-at-birth citizenship is recognition of existing status. Simplified naturalization for distant ancestry is discretionary and language-tested; it is not unconditional multi-generation jus sanguinis without process.

sources.title: Hungary Government — acquisition of Hungarian nationality

routes.title: Ordinary naturalization after continuous residence
routes.summary: Ordinary naturalization generally requires eight years of continuous residence in Hungary, clean criminal record, livelihood and housing, no public-security obstacle, and a constitutional exam in Hungarian, unless an exemption applies. Preferential shorter periods exist for spouses, parents of Hungarian minors and other statutory categories.

pathways.label: Ordinary naturalization after continuous residence
pathways.note: Residence eligibility is not an automatic grant. Simplified origin naturalization is modeled under the ancestry route family.

sources.title: Hungary Government — acquisition of Hungarian nationality

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through an Icelandic parent
routes.summary: Birth to an Icelandic parent creates Icelandic citizenship. Birth in Iceland alone to two foreign parents is not general jus soli.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through an Icelandic parent

routes.title: Icelandic citizenship through an Icelandic parent
routes.summary: A child of an Icelandic citizen parent is Icelandic under the Nationality Act. Separate routes exist for children of citizens living in Iceland and for restoration.

pathways.label: Icelandic citizenship through an Icelandic parent

routes.title: Citizenship after seven years residence
routes.summary: Adults generally need seven years continuous legal domicile in Iceland, Icelandic language, self-support, and good conduct. Shorter periods apply for Nordic citizens, spouses, refugees, and other statutory categories. Dual nationality is generally allowed.

pathways.label: Citizenship after seven years residence

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through an Indian parent
routes.summary: After 1987, birth in India creates citizenship only with a qualifying Indian parent (or later rules for both parents). Birth in India alone to two foreign parents is not general jus soli.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through an Indian parent

routes.title: Indian citizenship through an Indian parent
routes.summary: A child of an Indian citizen parent may be Indian under the Citizenship Act date rules. Overseas Citizen of India (OCI) is a separate status for persons of Indian origin abroad — not dual nationality.

pathways.label: Indian citizenship through an Indian parent
pathways.note: OCI is modeled in mobility as a diaspora status, not citizenship.

sources.title: India MEA — Overseas Citizenship of India Scheme

routes.title: Naturalization after long residence
routes.summary: Ordinary naturalization generally requires about twelve years residence (eleven of the prior fourteen plus twelve months immediately before applying), renunciation of prior nationality, and character conditions. Grant is discretionary.

pathways.label: Naturalization after long residence

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through an Indonesian parent
routes.summary: A person born to an Indonesian citizen parent acquires Indonesian citizenship under Law No. 12 of 2006. Birth in Indonesia alone to two foreign parents is not general jus soli.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through an Indonesian parent

sources.title: Indonesia Law No. 12 of 2006 on Citizenship (English text)

routes.title: Citizenship through an Indonesian parent
routes.summary: A child acquires Indonesian citizenship when a parent is an Indonesian citizen under the lineage rules of Law No. 12 of 2006, including specified mixed-nationality and acknowledgment situations. Limited dual citizenship for children ends when the child must choose nationality at majority or marriage.

pathways.label: Citizenship through an Indonesian parent

sources.title: Indonesia Law No. 12 of 2006 on Citizenship (English text)

routes.title: Naturalization after five consecutive or ten intermittent years
routes.summary: A foreign adult (or married applicant) may request naturalization after residing in Indonesian territory for at least five consecutive years or at least ten years intermittently, with capacity, Indonesian language ability, acceptance of Pancasila and the Constitution, good health, no serious criminal conviction, livelihood, payment of the statutory fee, and no dual citizenship upon becoming Indonesian. Approval is discretionary at the presidential level after ministerial process.

pathways.label: Naturalization after five consecutive or ten intermittent years
pathways.note: The five-year consecutive floor is the ordinary continuous path; the ten-year intermittent alternative is statutory. Special contribution naturalization under Article 20 is exceptional and not modeled as CBI.

sources.title: Indonesia Law No. 12 of 2006 on Citizenship (English text)

routes.title: Citizenship by birth on the island of Ireland
routes.summary: For births from 2005, citizenship depends on a parent’s citizenship, entitlement, or qualifying residence before the birth.

pathways.label: Irish citizen or entitled parent
Parent resident for three of the previous four years
pathways.note: Specified study and protection-processing periods do not count toward the parent’s residence calculation.

sources.title: Irish Nationality and Citizenship Act 2004 — Section 4

routes.title: Citizenship through an Irish parent or grandparent
routes.summary: Citizenship may be automatic through an Irish-born parent or available through Foreign Birth Registration in specified overseas cases.

pathways.label: Parent born on the island of Ireland
pathways.note: The Department of Foreign Affairs describes this category as automatic.

pathways.label: Foreign Birth Registration
pathways.note: Citizenship begins when the person is entered on the Foreign Births Register.

sources.title: Irish Department of Foreign Affairs — Born abroad

routes.title: Naturalization after reckonable residence
routes.summary: The standard adult route requires five years of reckonable residence in the previous nine, including one continuous year immediately before applying.

pathways.label: Standard adult naturalization
pathways.note: The Minister retains discretion and other statutory conditions apply.

sources.title: Irish Immigration Service — How to become an Irish citizen

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through an Israeli parent
routes.summary: A person born in Israel after the establishment of the State acquires Israeli nationality by birth when a parent was an Israeli national; birth in Israel alone to two foreign parents is not general jus soli under the Nationality Law.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through an Israeli parent

routes.title: Citizenship by return or through an Israeli parent
routes.summary: Every Jew has the right to come to Israel as an oleh under the Law of Return; the same rights extend to a child and grandchild of a Jew and to specified spouses, subject to the statutory exceptions. Separately, the Nationality Law transmits Israeli nationality through an Israeli mother or father.

pathways.label: Citizenship by return or through an Israeli parent
pathways.note: Law of Return eligibility is not ordinary multi-generation ethnic descent without the statutory Jewish/family definitions. Security, health and anti-Jewish-people activity exclusions apply.

routes.title: Naturalization after permanent residence
routes.summary: A non-national adult may naturalize while in Israel after three years of presence out of the five years before application, permanent-residence entitlement, settlement or intention to settle, some knowledge of Hebrew, and renunciation or loss of prior nationality upon naturalization.

pathways.label: Naturalization after permanent residence
pathways.note: Jewish olim under the Law of Return are not subject to the ordinary non-Jew renunciation pattern. Naturalization remains discretionary and security-screened.

routes.title: Citizenship through an Italian parent
routes.summary: A child of an Italian citizen can acquire citizenship by descent, but the 2025 reform limits automatic transmission to foreign-born people who hold another citizenship unless a statutory connection exception applies.

pathways.label: Italian parent and statutory connection

sources.title: Italian Ministry of Foreign Affairs — Citizenship

routes.title: Limited citizenship routes connected to birth in Italy
routes.summary: Birth in Italy is not generally enough. Citizenship exists at birth in limited statelessness or non-transmission cases, while a foreign child born and continuously resident in Italy may declare citizenship after reaching adulthood.

pathways.label: Otherwise stateless at birth
Born and continuously resident until adulthood
pathways.note: This is later acquisition because of birth and residence, not unconditional citizenship at birth.

sources.title: Italian Ministry of the Interior — Citizenship
Italian Ministry of Foreign Affairs — Citizenship

routes.title: Naturalization after legal residence
routes.summary: The general route permits an application after ten years of legal residence, with shorter periods for specified groups.

pathways.label: General ten-year route
pathways.note: Other statutory requirements and differentiated residence periods can apply.

sources.title: Italian Ministry of the Interior — Citizenship
Italian Ministry of Foreign Affairs — Citizenship

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through a Japanese parent or foundling rule
routes.summary: A person born to a Japanese father or mother is Japanese at birth. A person born in Japan whose parents are both unknown or without nationality is also Japanese. Birth in Japan alone to identified foreign parents is not general jus soli.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through a Japanese parent or foundling rule

sources.title: Japan Ministry of Justice — Nationality Q&A

routes.title: Japanese nationality through a Japanese parent
routes.summary: A child is a Japanese citizen if the father or mother is a Japanese citizen at the time of birth, or if the father died before birth while a Japanese citizen. Acknowledgment after birth by a Japanese father can support nationality acquisition by notification under Article 3 when statutory age and status conditions are met.

pathways.label: Japanese nationality through a Japanese parent
pathways.note: Children born abroad who also acquire a foreign nationality at birth must retain Japanese nationality within the Family Register Act period or may lose it retroactively, subject to reacquisition routes.

sources.title: Japan Ministry of Justice — Nationality Q&A

routes.title: Naturalization with permission of the Minister of Justice
routes.summary: A foreign national may acquire Japanese nationality by naturalization with permission of the Minister of Justice. Ordinary minimum conditions include five or more years continuous domicile in Japan, majority and capacity, good conduct, livelihood, and renunciation or loss of prior nationality, plus constitutional-compliance conditions. Meeting the statutory minima does not create an entitlement to permission.

pathways.label: Naturalization with permission of the Minister of Justice
pathways.note: Article 5 of the Nationality Act still states five years continuous domicile. Screening is discretionary and relaxed statutory periods exist for spouses, Japanese-born applicants and other special relationships under Articles 6–8. Administrative practice may apply stricter documentary or continuity expectations than the statutory floor.

sources.title: Japan Ministry of Justice — Nationality Q&A

routes.title: Limited citizenship connected to birth in Jordan
routes.summary: A person born in Jordan to a Jordanian mother and a father who is unknown, stateless or whose filiation is not established is Jordanian. A foundling in Jordan is presumed born there and is Jordanian unless the contrary is proved. Birth in Jordan to two foreign parents is not general jus soli.

pathways.label: Limited citizenship connected to birth in Jordan

sources.title: Jordanian Nationality Law No. 6 of 1954 (as amended)

routes.title: Jordanian nationality through a Jordanian father
routes.summary: A person whose father holds Jordanian nationality is Jordanian, and the children of a Jordanian man are Jordanian wherever they are born.

pathways.label: Jordanian nationality through a Jordanian father

sources.title: Jordanian Nationality Law No. 6 of 1954 (as amended)

routes.title: Investor citizenship criteria
routes.summary: Jordan's Cabinet-approved criteria, administered by the Ministry of Investment / Invest Jordan, provide citizenship routes through qualifying securities, productive projects, business expansion or sustained job creation. Several project routes require a multi-year compliance period, often with a temporary three-year passport before a recommendation for full citizenship. Dual nationality is routinely permitted on this economic pathway.

pathways.label: Investor citizenship criteria
pathways.note: Property purchase alone is a residence route, not direct citizenship. Passive deposit and treasury-bond routes have been phased out. Thresholds and employment numbers change by Cabinet decision; always verify the current Invest Jordan publication before relying on a quote. Origin-country dual-nationality policy should still be checked case by case.

sources.title: Invest Jordan — Cabinet investor residency and citizenship criteria
Jordan News Agency (Petra) — Cabinet investor citizenship amendments

routes.title: Naturalization after four years of regular residence
routes.summary: A non-Jordanian adult may apply to the Council of Ministers after four years of regular residence and an intention to reside in Jordan. The Cabinet may grant or refuse the certificate and may waive the residence period for an Arab applicant or in the public interest with royal approval. Ordinary naturalization under the Nationality Law requires loss of the prior nationality; this renunciation rule is distinct from the investor Cabinet pathway.

pathways.label: Naturalization after four years of regular residence
pathways.note: Eligibility to apply is not a right to citizenship. Article 13 of the Nationality Law conditions an ordinary certificate on loss of prior nationality. Political and public-office restrictions apply for years after naturalization. Do not assume the same renunciation rule for investor citizenship.

sources.title: Jordanian Nationality Law No. 6 of 1954 (as amended)

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through a Kenyan parent
routes.summary: Birth to a Kenyan citizen parent creates Kenyan citizenship by birth. Birth in Kenya alone to two foreign parents is not general jus soli.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through a Kenyan parent

routes.title: Kenyan citizenship through a Kenyan parent
routes.summary: A person is a citizen by birth if a parent is a Kenyan citizen at the time of birth.

pathways.label: Kenyan citizenship through a Kenyan parent

routes.title: Registration after seven years lawful residence
routes.summary: Adults lawfully resident for a continuous period of at least seven years may apply for registration as a citizen. Spouses of Kenyans may apply after seven years of marriage. Grant follows statutory conditions.

pathways.label: Registration after seven years lawful residence

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through a Korean parent
routes.summary: A person born to a Korean national parent acquires Korean nationality at birth. Birth in Korea alone to two foreign parents is not general jus soli under the Nationality Act.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through a Korean parent

routes.title: Citizenship through a Korean parent or simplified origin naturalization
routes.summary: A child acquires Korean nationality when a parent is a Korean national at birth. Separately, simplified naturalization routes exist for persons with a Korean parent, former Korean nationals, or other statutory family/origin links after shorter residence than ordinary naturalization, subject to Nationality Act conditions and Ministry of Justice screening.

pathways.label: Citizenship through a Korean parent or simplified origin naturalization
pathways.note: Parent-at-birth status is recognition of existing nationality. Simplified naturalization for adult applicants remains discretionary and is not automatic multi-generation jus sanguinis without process.

routes.title: General naturalization after five years domicile and permanent residence
routes.summary: General naturalization requires more than five consecutive years of domicile in the Republic of Korea, permanent residency status, legal majority under Korean civil law, good conduct, livelihood ability, basic Korean language and cultural knowledge, and no national-security or public-order obstacle. Permission is granted by the Minister of Justice; eligibility is not an entitlement.

pathways.label: General naturalization after five years domicile and permanent residence
pathways.note: Applicants generally must renounce prior nationality after permission, subject to dual-nationality exceptions for marriage migrants, outstanding talent and other Nationality Act categories. Spousal and origin simplified tracks use shorter residence floors.

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through a Latvian parent
routes.summary: Birth to a Latvian parent creates Latvian citizenship. Birth in Latvia alone to two foreign parents is not general jus soli.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through a Latvian parent

routes.title: Latvian citizenship through a Latvian parent
routes.summary: A child of a Latvian citizen parent is Latvian under the Citizenship Law. Separate registration routes exist for certain descendants of former citizens.

pathways.label: Latvian citizenship through a Latvian parent

routes.title: Naturalization after five years permanent residence
routes.summary: From age 15, applicants generally need five years permanent residence in Latvia, Latvian language and civics knowledge, livelihood, and loyalty. Dual nationality is limited to statutory categories. Grant follows examination and decision.

pathways.label: Naturalization after five years permanent residence

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through a Liechtenstein parent
routes.summary: Birth to a Liechtenstein parent creates Liechtenstein citizenship. Birth in Liechtenstein alone to two foreign parents is not general jus soli.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through a Liechtenstein parent

routes.title: Liechtenstein citizenship through a Liechtenstein parent
routes.summary: A child of a Liechtenstein citizen parent is Liechtenstein under nationality law, subject to registration rules for birth abroad.

pathways.label: Liechtenstein citizenship through a Liechtenstein parent

routes.title: Naturalization after long residence
routes.summary: Ordinary naturalization is highly discretionary and typically involves about ten years residence plus municipal approval. Facilitated naturalization after about thirty years residence (years under twenty count double) or marriage routes also exist. Renunciation of prior nationality is required.

pathways.label: Naturalization after long residence

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through a Lithuanian parent
routes.summary: Birth to a Lithuanian parent creates Lithuanian citizenship. Birth in Lithuania alone to two foreign parents is not general jus soli.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through a Lithuanian parent

routes.title: Lithuanian citizenship through a Lithuanian parent
routes.summary: A child of a Lithuanian citizen parent is Lithuanian. Separate restoration routes exist for pre-1940 citizens and their descendants.

pathways.label: Lithuanian citizenship through a Lithuanian parent

routes.title: Naturalization after ten years permanent residence
routes.summary: Adults generally need ten years permanent legal residence (seven if married to a Lithuanian), permanent residence status, Lithuanian language and Constitution exams, livelihood, and renunciation of prior nationality after grant. Decision is discretionary.

pathways.label: Naturalization after ten years permanent residence

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through a Luxembourgish parent
routes.summary: Birth to a Luxembourgish parent creates Luxembourgish nationality. Birth in Luxembourg alone to two foreign parents is not unrestricted jus soli.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through a Luxembourgish parent

routes.title: Luxembourgish nationality through a Luxembourgish parent
routes.summary: A child of a Luxembourgish parent is Luxembourgish under the nationality law. Option and reclamation routes cover additional family and historical cases.

pathways.label: Luxembourgish nationality through a Luxembourgish parent

routes.title: Naturalization after five years residence
routes.summary: Adults with five years legal residence (final year uninterrupted), Luxembourgish language certificate, and the Living Together course or exam may apply. Good repute rules apply. Dual nationality is generally allowed.

pathways.label: Naturalization after five years residence

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through a Malaysian parent
routes.summary: A person born to a Malaysian citizen parent is Malaysian under the Constitution’s operation-of-law and registration rules. Birth in Malaysia to two foreign parents is not general jus soli.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through a Malaysian parent

routes.title: Malaysian citizenship through a Malaysian parent
routes.summary: A child of a Malaysian citizen parent may hold or register Malaysian citizenship under the Federal Constitution, subject to the birthplace and registration rules in force.

pathways.label: Malaysian citizenship through a Malaysian parent

routes.title: Naturalization after long residence
routes.summary: Federal Constitution naturalization generally requires ten years residence within the twelve years before application, including twelve months immediately before applying, Malay language ability, good character, and renunciation of prior nationality. Approval is highly discretionary.

pathways.label: Naturalization after long residence

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through a qualifying parent
routes.summary: For births on or after 1 August 1989, birth in Malta confers citizenship only where a parent has the qualifying citizenship or status required by the Act.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through a qualifying parent

sources.title: Community Malta Agency — Acquisition of Citizenship

routes.title: Maltese citizenship by descent
routes.summary: Malta provides registration routes for specified family situations, including descendants in the direct line where the relevant line includes two consecutive ascendants born in Malta, and for certain second or subsequent generations born abroad. A foreign spouse of a Maltese citizen may also qualify for registration after five years of marriage, subject to the applicable conditions.

pathways.label: Maltese citizenship by descent

sources.title: Community Malta Agency — Acquisition of Citizenship

routes.title: Maltese naturalization by residence
routes.summary: An adult may apply after residing in Malta throughout the twelve months immediately before the application and for an aggregate of at least four years during the preceding six years. The applicant must also meet character and language requirements. Naturalization remains a ministerial, discretionary decision rather than an entitlement after five calendar years.

pathways.label: Maltese naturalization by residence
pathways.note: The statutory residence threshold establishes eligibility to apply, not an entitlement to citizenship.

sources.title: Community Malta Agency — Acquisition of Citizenship

routes.title: Former investor-citizenship programme
routes.summary: Malta discontinued the Granting of Citizenship for Exceptional Services programme following the Court of Justice of the European Union judgment. Current exceptional-merit naturalization is a discretionary route based on genuine exceptional service or contribution and residence; it should not be represented as a direct citizenship-by-investment product.

pathways.label: Former investor-citizenship programme
pathways.note: Historical route retained to state explicitly that transactional investor citizenship ended in 2025.

sources.title: Government of Malta — citizenship programme discontinued
Community Malta Agency — amendments to the Citizenship Act
Community Malta Agency — naturalisation on the basis of merit

routes.title: Citizenship through a Mauritian parent
routes.summary: A person born abroad is a Mauritian citizen by descent when a qualifying parent is a Mauritian citizen by birth.

pathways.label: Citizenship through a Mauritian parent

routes.title: Citizenship connected to birth in Mauritius
routes.summary: Birth in Mauritius before 1 October 1995 generally conferred citizenship regardless of parentage; after that date, at least one parent must be a Mauritian citizen.

pathways.label: Citizenship connected to birth in Mauritius

routes.title: Investor naturalization after residence
routes.summary: The Minister may accept two continuous years of residence from an applicant who has invested at least US$500,000 in Mauritius, instead of the ordinary residence pattern.

pathways.label: Investor naturalization after residence
pathways.note: This is discretionary naturalization with a statutory investment and residence condition, not residence-free direct citizenship.

routes.title: Naturalization after qualifying residence
routes.summary: A non-Commonwealth applicant may seek naturalization after twelve continuous months immediately before applying and five aggregate years during the preceding seven years, subject to character, language and residence-intention requirements.

pathways.label: Naturalization after qualifying residence

routes.title: Mexican nationality by territorial birth
routes.summary: A person born in Mexican territory is Mexican by birth regardless of the parents’ nationality; the Constitution also covers births aboard qualifying Mexican vessels and aircraft.

pathways.label: Mexican nationality by territorial birth

routes.title: Mexican nationality through a Mexican parent
routes.summary: Article 30 treats a person born abroad to a Mexican mother or father as Mexican by birth under the constitutional parentage rules.

pathways.label: Mexican nationality through a Mexican parent

routes.title: Mexican naturalization after residence
routes.summary: The ordinary route requires five years of residence. Article 20 reduces the period to two years for a person originating from Latin America or the Iberian Peninsula and for a parent of a Mexican child by birth.

pathways.label: Ordinary five-year residence route
Two-year route for Latin American or Iberian Peninsula origin
Two-year route for a parent of a Mexican child by birth
pathways.note: The child is Mexican at birth. Parents and grandparents can qualify for permanent residence through the direct family relationship, but only a parent with a Mexican child by birth receives this two-year naturalization route; citizenship is not automatic for either generation.

sources.title: Mexico Foreign Ministry — naturalization for parents of Mexican-born children
Mexico INM — permanent residence through family relationship

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through a Monegasque parent
routes.summary: Birth to a Monegasque parent creates Monegasque nationality. Birth in Monaco alone to two foreign parents is not general jus soli.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through a Monegasque parent

sources.title: Monaco mon service public - acquiring nationality

routes.title: Monegasque nationality through a Monegasque parent
routes.summary: A child of a Monegasque parent is Monegasque under nationality law, with detailed maternal and paternal transmission rules.

pathways.label: Monegasque nationality through a Monegasque parent

sources.title: Monaco mon service public - acquiring nationality

routes.title: Naturalization after ten years residence
routes.summary: Adults with ordinary residence in Monaco for at least ten years after age eighteen may petition the Sovereign Prince. Naturalization is discretionary and requires renunciation of prior nationality and freedom from foreign military service obligations.

pathways.label: Naturalization after ten years residence

sources.title: Monaco mon service public - acquiring nationality

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through a Moroccan parent
routes.summary: Birth to a Moroccan parent creates Moroccan nationality. Birth in Morocco alone to two foreign parents is not general jus soli.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through a Moroccan parent

routes.title: Moroccan nationality through a Moroccan parent
routes.summary: A child of a Moroccan father or mother is Moroccan under the nationality code.

pathways.label: Moroccan nationality through a Moroccan parent

routes.title: Naturalization after five years residence
routes.summary: Foreign adults may apply after five years continuous legal residence, with language and character conditions. Shorter tracks exist for spouses. Grant is discretionary.

pathways.label: Naturalization after five years residence

routes.title: Citizenship by descent through a Nauruan parent
routes.summary: A person born outside Nauru is a citizen if, at the time of birth, either parent was a citizen.

pathways.label: Citizenship by descent through a Nauruan parent

routes.title: Citizenship connected to birth in Nauru
routes.summary: A person born in Nauru is a citizen at birth if either parent is a citizen. A person born in Nauru to non-citizen parents may later apply after twenty continuous years of residence. Foundling and otherwise-stateless birth situations are also protected under the constitutional and statutory framework.

pathways.label: Citizenship connected to birth in Nauru
pathways.note: The twenty-year route is later discretionary acquisition connected to birthplace, not citizenship at birth.

routes.title: Economic and Climate Resilience Citizenship Program
routes.summary: A statutory contribution programme administered by the Nauru Program Office under the Nauru Economic and Climate Resilience Citizenship Act 2024, under which a foreign adult may acquire economic and climate resilience citizenship after a prescribed contribution and due diligence, without a residence requirement.

pathways.label: Economic and Climate Resilience Citizenship Program
pathways.note: The 2024 Act creates the no-residence contribution framework; contribution amounts are programme-prescribed rather than a permanent single figure in the Act. Published baselines have been in the region of USD 115,000, with limited-time promotional discounts (for example lower temporary tiers). Verify current Program Office figures and due-diligence fees before relying on a quote.

sources.title: Nauru Economic and Climate Resilience Citizenship Act 2024
Nauru Program Office — Economic and Climate Resilience Citizenship Program
Government of Nauru — climate resilience citizenship programme launch

routes.title: Naturalization through marriage to a Nauruan citizen
routes.summary: A foreign spouse may apply after seven continuous years of lawful marriage and residence with the Nauruan spouse, subject to character, health, commitment to reside and no-criminal-conviction conditions. Cabinet may waive the residence period.

pathways.label: Naturalization through marriage to a Nauruan citizen
pathways.note: The grant is made by the Minister in consultation with Cabinet and is final without court appeal under the Act.

routes.title: Dutch citizenship through a parent
routes.summary: A child generally acquires Dutch citizenship automatically when the statutory Dutch-parent conditions are met at birth.

pathways.label: Dutch parent at birth

sources.title: IND — Dutch citizen by birth, acknowledgment or adoption

routes.title: Naturalization after legal residence
routes.summary: The standard naturalization route generally requires five consecutive years of lawful residence with a qualifying status.

pathways.label: Standard five-year route
