pathways.note: Status, integration, public-order, and usually renunciation requirements also apply; statutory exceptions can shorten the term.

routes.title: Automatic citizenship for a qualifying third generation
routes.summary: A child can acquire Dutch citizenship at birth under the statutory third-generation principal-residence rule even without a Dutch parent.

pathways.label: Third-generation principal-residence rule

sources.title: IND — Dutch citizen by birth, acknowledgment or adoption

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through a Vanuatu parent
routes.summary: A child born after independence acquires citizenship at birth, whether born in Vanuatu or abroad, if at least one parent is a citizen.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through a Vanuatu parent

routes.title: Citizenship through a Vanuatu parent
routes.summary: A person born after independence, in Vanuatu or abroad, becomes a citizen when at least one parent is a Vanuatu citizen.

pathways.label: Citizenship through a Vanuatu parent

routes.title: Investor and contribution citizenship programmes
routes.summary: The Citizenship Commission lists investor citizenship, the Development Support Program, the Vanuatu Contribution Program and a real-estate option under the Citizenship Act framework.

pathways.label: Investor and contribution citizenship programmes
pathways.note: Programme options, contribution levels, due-diligence rules and designated agents are volatile. Verify them against current Commission regulations before relying on a quote.

sources.title: Vanuatu Citizenship Commission — Types of Citizenship

routes.title: Naturalization after continuous residence
routes.summary: A foreign national or stateless person may apply after living continuously in Vanuatu for at least ten years, subject to further statutory conditions and review.

pathways.label: Naturalization after continuous residence

routes.title: Citizenship by birth in New Zealand
routes.summary: For births from 1 January 2006, a child born in New Zealand is a citizen when at least one parent is a citizen or has the right to live in New Zealand indefinitely.

pathways.label: Citizen or indefinitely resident parent

sources.title: New Zealand Government — Citizenship by birth, descent and grant

routes.title: Citizenship by descent
routes.summary: A person born overseas can register as a citizen by descent when a parent was a New Zealand citizen by birth or grant at the time of birth.

pathways.label: Parent citizen by birth or grant
pathways.note: A citizen by descent generally cannot transmit citizenship to another overseas-born generation.

sources.title: New Zealand Government — Citizenship by birth, descent and grant

routes.title: Citizenship by grant after residence
routes.summary: Most adults need five years as a resident, 1,350 days of physical presence in total, and at least 240 days in each of those five years.

pathways.label: Standard five-year presence route
pathways.note: Language, character, and intention-to-reside requirements also apply.

sources.title: New Zealand Government — Citizenship presence requirements
New Zealand Government — Citizenship by birth, descent and grant

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through a Nigerian parent or grandparent rules
routes.summary: A person born outside Nigeria either of whose parents is a citizen of Nigeria is a citizen by birth. Birth in Nigeria after independence with a citizen parent or grandparent also creates citizenship by birth under section 25. Birth in Nigeria alone to two foreign parents without qualifying lineage is not general jus soli.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through a Nigerian parent or grandparent rules

sources.title: Nigeria Constitution 1999 — Chapter III Citizenship (published text)
Constitute Project — Constitution of Nigeria 1999 (as amended)

routes.title: Citizenship by birth through a Nigerian parent
routes.summary: Under section 25 of the 1999 Constitution, a person is a citizen of Nigeria by birth if born outside Nigeria to a Nigerian citizen parent, or if born in Nigeria after independence to a parent or grandparent who is a citizen, subject to the indigenous-community and historical rules for pre-independence births.

pathways.label: Citizenship by birth through a Nigerian parent
pathways.note: Registration under section 26 is a separate discretionary channel for certain spouses and persons of Nigerian origin and is not collapsed into ordinary naturalization.

sources.title: Nigeria Constitution 1999 — Chapter III Citizenship (published text)
Constitute Project — Constitution of Nigeria 1999 (as amended)

routes.title: Naturalization after fifteen years continuous residence
routes.summary: Section 27 of the 1999 Constitution allows the President to grant a certificate of naturalization to a person who satisfies the constitutional qualifications, including continuous residence in Nigeria for a period of fifteen years, majority, good character, intention to reside, community acceptability, and capacity to make a useful contribution. Section 28 requires renunciation of other nationalities except citizenship of a country acquired by birth. The Ministry of Interior administers applications; grant is discretionary.

pathways.label: Naturalization after fifteen years continuous residence
pathways.note: Interior guidance lists documentary requirements for naturalization under section 27. Registration under section 26 (including certain spouses) uses different constitutional criteria and is not modeled as a separate principal route here.

sources.title: Nigeria Constitution 1999 — Chapter III Citizenship (published text)
Nigeria Ministry of Interior — Citizenship requirements PDF
Constitute Project — Constitution of Nigeria 1999 (as amended)

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through a Norwegian parent
routes.summary: Birth to a Norwegian parent creates Norwegian citizenship. Birth in Norway alone to two foreign parents is not general jus soli.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through a Norwegian parent

routes.title: Norwegian citizenship through a Norwegian parent
routes.summary: A child of a Norwegian citizen parent is Norwegian under the Nationality Act.

pathways.label: Norwegian citizenship through a Norwegian parent

routes.title: Citizenship after residence and language requirements
routes.summary: Adults generally need a long period of continuous residence (commonly eight years within the last eleven), permanent residence, Norwegian language, social studies, and good conduct. Dual nationality is now generally permitted. Grant follows statutory conditions.

pathways.label: Citizenship after residence and language requirements

routes.title: Naturalization through a qualifying family connection
routes.summary: The Constitution reduces the residence period to three consecutive years for an applicant with a child born in Panama, a Panamanian parent, or a Panamanian spouse, while retaining the other Article 10 requirements.

pathways.label: Naturalization through a qualifying family connection

sources.title: Constitution of Panama — nationality and naturalization

routes.title: Nationality by birth in Panama
routes.summary: A person born in Panama is Panamanian by birth under Article 9 of the Constitution.

pathways.label: Nationality by birth in Panama

sources.title: Constitution of Panama — nationality and naturalization

routes.title: Nationality through a Panamanian parent
routes.summary: A person born abroad to a Panamanian parent can acquire nationality under Article 9, subject to domicile in Panama and, for a naturalized parent, a timely declaration after majority.

pathways.label: Nationality through a Panamanian parent

sources.title: Constitution of Panama — nationality and naturalization

routes.title: Ordinary naturalization
routes.summary: A permanent resident may apply after five consecutive years in Panama, subject to the constitutional declaration, nationality-renunciation, Spanish and civic-knowledge requirements.

pathways.label: Ordinary naturalization

sources.title: Constitution of Panama — nationality and naturalization

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through a PNG parent
routes.summary: Birth to a PNG citizen parent creates PNG citizenship. Birth in PNG alone to two foreign parents is not general jus soli.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through a PNG parent

routes.title: PNG citizenship through a PNG parent
routes.summary: A child of a Papua New Guinean citizen parent is a citizen under the Constitution and Citizenship Act, subject to registration rules for birth abroad.

pathways.label: PNG citizenship through a PNG parent

routes.title: Investor naturalization category
routes.summary: PNG ICA lists an investor naturalization request form as a discretionary ministerial track. It is not a published fixed-price citizenship-by-investment programme and still depends on statutory naturalization conditions and Cabinet or ministerial approval.

pathways.label: Investor naturalization category

sources.title: PNG ICA - citizenship and dual citizenship

routes.title: Naturalization after eight years residence
routes.summary: Adults with eight years continuous residence may apply. Applicants must show character, language or vernacular knowledge, respect for customs, self-support, civic knowledge, and generally renounce other citizenship unless dual nationality with a prescribed country is approved. Ministerial grant is discretionary.

pathways.label: Naturalization after eight years residence

sources.title: PNG ICA - citizenship and dual citizenship

routes.title: Natural nationality by birth in Paraguay
routes.summary: Persons born in the territory of the Republic are natural Paraguayans, subject to the constitutional exception for children of persons in the service of a foreign government.

pathways.label: Natural nationality by birth in Paraguay

routes.title: Natural nationality through a Paraguayan parent
routes.summary: Children of a Paraguayan mother or father born abroad are natural Paraguayans when they settle permanently in the Republic, or when a parent was in the service of the Republic at the time of birth abroad.

pathways.label: Natural nationality through a Paraguayan parent

sources.title: Paraguay Repatriados — inclusión a la identidad nacional

routes.title: Naturalization after minimum residence
routes.summary: Foreign adults may seek Paraguayan nationality by naturalization after a constitutional minimum of three years of residence in the national territory, majority, good conduct, and exercise of a profession, trade, science, art or industry, or ownership of property in the country, subject to the statutory process.

pathways.label: Naturalization after minimum residence
pathways.note: The three-year constitutional minimum is eligibility to apply, not an automatic grant. Administrative practice may involve temporary then permanent residence sequencing before the citizenship petition.

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through a Peruvian parent or birth in Peru
routes.summary: Birth to a Peruvian parent, or birth in Peru under the Nationality Law, creates Peruvian nationality. Ordinary foreign-parent temporary presence is not a general unrestricted jus soli path.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through a Peruvian parent or birth in Peru

routes.title: Peruvian nationality through a Peruvian parent
routes.summary: A child of a Peruvian parent is Peruvian under the Nationality Law lineage rules, including birth abroad subject to registration.

pathways.label: Peruvian nationality through a Peruvian parent

routes.title: Naturalization after two years legal residence
routes.summary: Adult foreigners may apply after at least two consecutive years of legal residence, subject to Migraciones conditions (means, good conduct, exam) for their migration quality. Marriage to a Peruvian has a parallel two-year continuous residence track.

pathways.label: Naturalization after two years legal residence

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through a Filipino parent
routes.summary: A person born to a Filipino father or mother is a Filipino citizen under the 1987 Constitution. Birth in the Philippines alone to two foreign parents does not generally create citizenship by soil.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through a Filipino parent

sources.title: Philippines Commonwealth Act No. 473 — Revised Naturalization Law

routes.title: Citizenship through a Filipino parent or natural-born reacquisition
routes.summary: A person is a Filipino citizen when at least one parent is a Filipino citizen, subject to constitutional and statutory lineage rules. Natural-born Filipinos who lost Philippine citizenship by naturalization abroad may reacquire and retain dual citizenship under Republic Act No. 9225 by taking the oath of allegiance and meeting documentary requirements.

pathways.label: Citizenship through a Filipino parent or natural-born reacquisition
pathways.note: RA 9225 reacquisition is for natural-born Filipinos who previously lost citizenship; it is not a general multi-generation registration without lineage proof.

sources.title: Philippines Commonwealth Act No. 473 — Revised Naturalization Law
Office of the Solicitor General — Special Committee on Naturalization

routes.title: Naturalization by judicial petition or administrative RA 9139 process
routes.summary: Ordinary naturalization for foreign adults is available by judicial petition under Commonwealth Act No. 473 after continuous residence of not less than ten years (with shorter statutory periods for specified categories), good moral character, means of support, language and civics knowledge, and other qualifications and disqualifications. Separately, Republic Act No. 9139 provides an administrative naturalization path through the Special Committee on Naturalization for persons born in the Philippines who meet native-born residence and other statutory conditions.

pathways.label: Naturalization by judicial petition or administrative RA 9139 process
pathways.note: CA 473 is the general judicial track; RA 9139 is a narrower administrative track for persons born and residing in the Philippines. Neither route is an entitlement after residence alone.

sources.title: Philippines Commonwealth Act No. 473 — Revised Naturalization Law
Office of the Solicitor General — Special Committee on Naturalization

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through a Polish parent
routes.summary: A person born to a Polish citizen parent is a Polish citizen at birth. Birth in Poland to two foreign parents does not generally create citizenship by soil alone.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through a Polish parent

routes.title: Polish citizenship through a Polish parent
routes.summary: A child acquires Polish citizenship when at least one parent is a Polish citizen, regardless of the place of birth, subject to the historical continuity rules of the Citizenship Act for older generations.

pathways.label: Polish citizenship through a Polish parent

routes.title: Recognition as a Polish citizen after permanent residence
routes.summary: A foreign national may be recognised as a Polish citizen after continuous legal residence of at least three years on permanent residence, EU long-term residence or permanent-residence right, with a stable income, a legal title to dwelling, and Polish language at B1. Shorter periods apply for spouses of Polish citizens, refugees, Polish origin / Karta Polaka holders and other statutory categories.

pathways.label: Recognition as a Polish citizen after permanent residence
pathways.note: Recognition may be refused for defence or public-security reasons. Presidential grant of citizenship is a separate discretionary channel with different documentary rules.

sources.title: Poland MSWiA — recognition as a Polish citizen

routes.title: Conditional citizenship through birth in Portugal
routes.summary: The 2026 nationality-law changes provide a birth-in-Portugal route where, at the time of birth, one parent has legally resided in Portugal for at least five years. This is a conditional jus soli rule, not unconditional citizenship for every child born in Portugal; additional statutory conditions and implementation rules must be checked for an individual case.

pathways.label: Parent with five years of legal residence
pathways.note: The five-year period applies to the parent before the child’s birth.

sources.title: Portuguese Ministry of Justice — Nationality Law: new rules effective 19 May

routes.title: Citizenship through a Portuguese parent
routes.summary: A person born abroad can obtain Portuguese nationality when a parent was Portuguese at the time of birth.

pathways.label: Portuguese parent at birth
pathways.note: Eligibility is by descent; registration and processing time are separate.

sources.title: Portuguese Ministry of Justice — Portuguese nationality
Portuguese Ministry of Justice — Submit a nationality application

routes.title: Ordinary naturalization after legal residence
routes.summary: For applications made from 19 May 2026, the ordinary legal-residence period is seven years for nationals of Portuguese-speaking countries and EU Member States, and ten years for other nationals. Applications already pending when the law entered into force remain under the earlier rules. The Ministry of Justice notes that some practical implementation still requires supplementary regulation; eligibility is not an automatic grant.

pathways.label: CPLP or EU national
pathways.note: Eligibility period for applications under the 2026 law.

pathways.label: Other national
pathways.note: General eligibility period for applications under the 2026 law.

sources.title: Portuguese Ministry of Justice — Nationality Law: new rules effective 19 May
Portuguese Ministry of Justice — Portuguese nationality

routes.title: Original citizenship at birth through a Timorese parent
routes.summary: Birth to a Timorese parent creates original citizenship under the Constitution. Additional birth-in-territory rules apply for certain parentage and declaration cases.

pathways.label: Original citizenship at birth through a Timorese parent

routes.title: Timorese citizenship through a Timorese parent
routes.summary: A child of a Timorese father or mother is an original citizen under the Constitution, including many births abroad in the direct line.

pathways.label: Timorese citizenship through a Timorese parent

routes.title: Naturalization after ten years residence
routes.summary: Adults generally need usual and regular residence for at least ten years after 20 May 2002 (or qualifying pre-1975 residence), official language ability, livelihood, good character, and knowledge of Timorese history and culture. Occupation-era transmigration residence does not count. Grant is discretionary.

pathways.label: Naturalization after ten years residence

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through a Romanian parent
routes.summary: Birth to a Romanian parent creates Romanian citizenship. Birth in Romania alone to two foreign parents is not general jus soli.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through a Romanian parent

routes.title: Romanian citizenship through a Romanian parent
routes.summary: A child of a Romanian citizen parent is Romanian. Separate restoration and origin routes exist for descendants of former citizens.

pathways.label: Romanian citizenship through a Romanian parent

routes.title: Naturalization after eight years residence
routes.summary: Adults generally need eight years legal residence (five if married to a Romanian), Romanian language and culture knowledge, livelihood, and good conduct. Dual nationality is generally allowed. Grant is discretionary.

pathways.label: Naturalization after eight years residence

routes.title: Citizenship by birth in St Kitts and Nevis
routes.summary: A person born in the federation acquires citizenship at birth, except for the Constitution’s diplomatic-immunity and hostile-occupation exceptions.

pathways.label: Citizenship by birth in St Kitts and Nevis

sources.title: Constitution of St Christopher and Nevis

routes.title: Citizenship through a citizen parent
routes.summary: The Constitution provides citizenship by descent for a person born abroad to a qualifying citizen parent.

pathways.label: Citizenship through a citizen parent

sources.title: Constitution of St Christopher and Nevis
St Christopher and Nevis Citizenship Act

routes.title: Citizenship Programme
routes.summary: Government-administered direct citizenship programme requiring a prescribed investment or contribution, including the Sustainable Island State Contribution (SISC), and due diligence.

pathways.label: Citizenship Programme
pathways.note: Official contribution/investment minimums currently start at US$250,000 for the SISC or Public Benefit option for a single principal applicant. Approved real-estate routes are higher. Due diligence, biometrics and passport fees are additional and change by circular; re-check the Citizenship Unit schedule before quoting all-in cost.

sources.title: St. Kitts and Nevis Citizenship Unit

routes.title: Naturalization after a fifteen-year qualifying window
routes.summary: The Citizenship Act requires residence throughout the twelve months immediately before application and residence during the preceding fourteen years, with good-character and intention-to-reside requirements.

pathways.label: Naturalization after a fifteen-year qualifying window
pathways.note: The Second Schedule states a final continuous 12 months plus residence during the 14 years before that period. This is not modeled as a simple 14-year continuous-residence rule.

sources.title: St Christopher and Nevis Citizenship Act

routes.title: Citizenship by Investment Programme
routes.summary: Direct citizenship by registration following a qualifying investment under the Citizenship by Investment Act.

pathways.label: Citizenship by Investment Programme
pathways.note: The National Economic Fund option currently starts at US$240,000 for a principal applicant with up to three qualifying dependants; other options and fees differ.

sources.title: Saint Lucia Citizenship by Investment Act

routes.title: Citizenship by birth in Saint Lucia
routes.summary: A person born in Saint Lucia acquires citizenship at birth, subject to the statutory diplomatic-immunity and hostile-occupation exceptions.

pathways.label: Citizenship by birth in Saint Lucia

routes.title: Citizenship through a citizen parent or grandparent
routes.summary: The 2024 amendment extends citizenship by descent to a person born abroad whose parent or grandparent is a Saint Lucian citizen by birth, subject to the statutory conditions.

pathways.label: Citizenship through a citizen parent or grandparent

sources.title: Saint Lucia Home Affairs — descent amendment

routes.title: Registration after seven years for a Commonwealth citizen
routes.summary: A Commonwealth citizen is entitled to apply for registration after seven years of ordinary residence in Saint Lucia, subject to the Constitution’s application and public-interest conditions.

pathways.label: Registration after seven years for a Commonwealth citizen

routes.title: Citizenship connected to birth in São Tomé and Príncipe
routes.summary: A person born in São Tomé and Príncipe is of origin nationality when a parent is São-tomense, when the parents are stateless or of unknown nationality, or when foreign parents reside in the territory and are not in the service of another state. Foundling and parental-status conditions remain statutory.

pathways.label: Citizenship connected to birth in São Tomé and Príncipe

sources.title: São Tomé and Príncipe — Lei n.º 07/2022 Lei da Nacionalidade

routes.title: Citizenship through a São-tomense parent or grandparent
routes.summary: Origin nationality covers a person born abroad to a São-tomense parent who declares the wish to be São-tomense, and descendants who are grandchildren of a São-tomense national born abroad.

pathways.label: Citizenship through a São-tomense parent or grandparent

sources.title: São Tomé and Príncipe — Lei n.º 07/2022 Lei da Nacionalidade

routes.title: Naturalization after five years of legal residence
routes.summary: The Government may naturalize a foreign adult or stateless person after at least five years of legal residence, Portuguese or national-language knowledge, clean criminal record, national-security clearance and means of subsistence. The grant is by government decree after a Public Prosecutor prior review.

pathways.label: Naturalization after five years of legal residence
pathways.note: Naturalization is discretionary and may be refused even when the formal residence period is met. Dual-nationality caps apply to some naturalized persons.

sources.title: São Tomé and Príncipe — Lei n.º 07/2022 Lei da Nacionalidade

routes.title: Citizenship by Investment Programme
routes.summary: A direct contribution route created under Decreto-Lei n.º 07/2025 as a special naturalization mechanism under Lei n.º 07/2022 for investment or donation to the National Transformation Fund, administered through the Citizenship by Investment and Donation Unit.

pathways.label: Citizenship by Investment Programme
pathways.note: The regulation frames the programme as naturalization for investment or donation, not residence-free passport purchase without process. Minimum contribution figures are set in the regulation annex and programme materials and should be re-checked before reliance.

sources.title: São Tomé and Príncipe — Decreto-Lei n.º 07/2025 RNID
São Tomé and Príncipe — Lei n.º 07/2022 Lei da Nacionalidade
Honorary Consulate of São Tomé and Príncipe — Citizenship by Investment

routes.title: Admission after permanent residence
routes.summary: An adult foreigner with permanent residence may request admission to Serbian citizenship subject to release or qualifying assurance regarding former citizenship and a declaration that Serbia is their state.

pathways.label: Admission after permanent residence
pathways.note: Current foreigner rules generally make permanent residence available after three years of continuous temporary residence; citizenship admission then remains a separate decision.

routes.title: Citizenship at birth to prevent statelessness
routes.summary: A child born or found in Serbia acquires citizenship where both parents are unknown, of unknown citizenship or stateless, or where the child would otherwise be stateless.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth to prevent statelessness

routes.title: Serbian citizenship by descent
routes.summary: Serbian citizenship is acquired by descent under the parentage and, for some foreign-born children, registration conditions in the Citizenship Act.

pathways.label: Serbian citizenship by descent

routes.title: Citizenship application after permanent residence
routes.summary: An adult permanent resident is eligible to apply after at least two years as a PR; approval remains discretionary.

pathways.label: Adult permanent resident
pathways.note: Eligibility to apply is not an entitlement to approval; national-service and other rules can apply.

sources.title: Singapore ICA — Becoming a Singapore citizen
Constitution of the Republic of Singapore — Citizenship

routes.title: Citizenship by birth in Singapore
routes.summary: A person born in Singapore is a citizen by birth when at least one parent is a Singapore citizen, subject to the constitutional exceptions.

pathways.label: Born in Singapore to a citizen parent

sources.title: Constitution of the Republic of Singapore — Citizenship

routes.title: Citizenship by descent
routes.summary: A person born outside Singapore can acquire citizenship by descent through a Singapore citizen parent, subject to registration and additional connection rules where the parent is also a citizen by descent.

pathways.label: Singapore citizen parent
pathways.note: A citizen-by-descent parent must also satisfy the Constitution’s Singapore-residence connection test.

sources.title: Constitution of the Republic of Singapore — Citizenship
Singapore ICA — Becoming a Singapore citizen

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through a Slovak parent
routes.summary: Birth to a Slovak parent creates Slovak citizenship. Birth in Slovakia alone to two foreign parents is not general jus soli.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through a Slovak parent

routes.title: Slovak citizenship through a Slovak parent
routes.summary: A child of a Slovak citizen parent is Slovak. Descent and restoration routes also cover certain Czechoslovak-line ancestors and former citizens.

pathways.label: Slovak citizenship through a Slovak parent

routes.title: Naturalization after eight years permanent residence
routes.summary: Adults generally need eight years continuous permanent residence immediately before applying, Slovak language knowledge, livelihood, and good conduct. Shorter paths exist for spouses and some descent categories. Grant is discretionary.

pathways.label: Naturalization after eight years permanent residence

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through a Vietnamese parent
routes.summary: Birth to a Vietnamese parent creates Vietnamese nationality. Birth in Vietnam alone to two foreign parents is not general jus soli.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through a Vietnamese parent

routes.title: Vietnamese nationality through a Vietnamese parent
routes.summary: A child of a Vietnamese citizen parent is Vietnamese under the Nationality Law.

pathways.label: Vietnamese nationality through a Vietnamese parent

routes.title: Naturalization after five years residence
routes.summary: Adult foreigners may apply after five consecutive years of permanent residence, with language, livelihood, and character conditions. Applicants generally renounce prior nationality. Grant is discretionary.

pathways.label: Naturalization after five years residence

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through a South African parent
routes.summary: A person born to a South African citizen parent is a South African citizen by birth under the Citizenship Act. Birth in the Republic to two foreign parents is not unconditional jus soli; specified later naturalisation or registration routes may apply in limited statutory cases.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through a South African parent

sources.title: South African Citizenship Act 88 of 1995 (gov.za PDF)
South African Citizenship Amendment Act 17 of 2010

routes.title: Citizenship through a South African parent or descent
routes.summary: A person is a South African citizen by birth or descent when a parent is a South African citizen under the Citizenship Act, including citizenship transmitted through a citizen parent regardless of birthplace in the ordinary jus sanguinis cases, subject to registration and historical continuity rules.

pathways.label: Citizenship through a South African parent or descent

sources.title: South African Citizenship Act 88 of 1995 (gov.za PDF)
South African Citizenship Amendment Act 17 of 2010

routes.title: Naturalisation after permanent residence and ordinary residence
routes.summary: The Minister may grant a certificate of naturalisation to an adult foreigner who has been admitted for permanent residence and has been ordinarily resident in the Republic for a continuous period of not less than five years immediately preceding the application (as amended by Act 17 of 2010), is of good character, intends continued residence or qualifying service, can communicate in an official language, and has adequate knowledge of the responsibilities and privileges of citizenship. Naturalisation remains discretionary.

pathways.label: Naturalisation after permanent residence and ordinary residence
pathways.note: Regulations may impose additional presence limits within the five-year ordinary-residence period. Spousal and exceptional ministerial routes exist with different residence floors.

sources.title: South African Citizenship Act 88 of 1995 (gov.za PDF)
South African Citizenship Amendment Act 17 of 2010
South African Government — Citizenship Act document page

routes.title: Citizenship through birth in Spain
routes.summary: Birth in Spain alone does not generally confer citizenship. Nationality by origin applies in limited parentage, statelessness, or undetermined-parentage cases.

pathways.label: Foreign parent also born in Spain
No nationality transmitted by either parent
Undetermined parentage
pathways.note: Spanish origin nationality applies when parentage is undetermined; the Civil Code also presumes certain found minors were born in Spain.

routes.title: Citizenship through a Spanish parent or option
routes.summary: Spain recognizes nationality by origin through a Spanish parent and an option route for some children of originally Spanish parents.

pathways.label: Spanish parent
pathways.note: Nationality by origin; documentation time is separate.

pathways.label: Option through an originally Spanish parent born in Spain
pathways.note: A statutory option route under Civil Code Article 20.

routes.title: Naturalization after legal residence
routes.summary: The general residence period is ten years and five years for recognized refugees. A person born in Spain who is not already Spanish by origin can apply after one year of legal, continuous residence immediately before applying; the grant is not automatic.

pathways.label: Ordinary residence
pathways.note: Eligibility period only; the grant is not automatic.

pathways.label: Recognized refugee
pathways.note: Five-year residence period for a person recognized as a refugee.

pathways.label: Born in Spain
pathways.note: One year of legal, continuous residence immediately before the application. Article 22 also requires good civic conduct and sufficient integration; the grant is not automatic.

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through a Swedish parent
routes.summary: Birth to a Swedish parent creates Swedish citizenship. Birth in Sweden alone to two foreign parents is not general jus soli.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through a Swedish parent

sources.title: Swedish Migration Agency - citizenship for adults

routes.title: Swedish citizenship through a Swedish parent
routes.summary: A child of a Swedish citizen parent is Swedish under the Citizenship Act.

pathways.label: Swedish citizenship through a Swedish parent

sources.title: Swedish Migration Agency - citizenship for adults

routes.title: Citizenship after eight years residence
routes.summary: Adults generally need continuous residence of at least eight years, permanent residence status, identity proof, and good conduct. Shorter periods apply for Nordic citizens, spouses, and other statutory categories. Language and knowledge requirements apply under the current rules.

pathways.label: Citizenship after eight years residence

sources.title: Swedish Migration Agency - citizenship for adults

routes.title: Swiss citizenship through a parent
routes.summary: Swiss citizenship is principally acquired through paternal or maternal descent rather than birthplace alone.

pathways.label: Swiss parent

routes.title: Ordinary naturalization
routes.summary: Ordinary naturalization generally requires ten years in Switzerland and a permanent C permit, alongside federal, cantonal, and communal requirements.

pathways.label: Ten-year route with C permit
pathways.note: Three years must fall within the five years before filing; cantonal and communal residence rules also apply.

routes.title: Facilitated naturalization for a third generation born in Switzerland
routes.summary: Birth in Switzerland does not itself grant citizenship, but a qualifying third-generation applicant born there may use facilitated naturalization before age 25.

pathways.label: Third generation born in Switzerland
pathways.note: This is later facilitated naturalization connected to birthplace and family residence, not citizenship at birth.

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through a Thai parent
routes.summary: A person born to a Thai national parent is a Thai national at birth under the Nationality Act. Birth in Thailand alone to two foreign parents is not unconditional jus soli.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through a Thai parent

sources.title: Thailand Nationality Act B.E. 2508 (amended; UNHCR English text)

routes.title: Thai nationality through a Thai parent
routes.summary: A person acquires Thai nationality by birth when a parent is a Thai national under the Nationality Act lineage rules, including specified transmission through a Thai father or mother and related acknowledgment situations.

pathways.label: Thai nationality through a Thai parent

sources.title: Thailand Nationality Act B.E. 2508 (amended; UNHCR English text)

routes.title: Naturalization with ministerial and Cabinet approval
routes.summary: A foreigner may be naturalized as a Thai national with permission of the Minister of Interior and Cabinet approval after meeting the statutory conditions of the Nationality Act, which ordinarily include majority and capacity, good conduct, lawful occupation, knowledge of Thai sufficient for communication, and domicile in Thailand for not less than five consecutive years under a permanent-residence qualification, unless a shorter statutory path applies (for example certain spouses of Thai nationals). Naturalization remains highly discretionary.

pathways.label: Naturalization with ministerial and Cabinet approval
pathways.note: Permanent residence is itself discretionary and quota-limited. Marriage to a Thai national may shorten residence floors but does not create automatic citizenship.

sources.title: Thailand Nationality Act B.E. 2508 (amended; UNHCR English text)

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through a Tongan parent
routes.summary: Birth to a Tongan parent creates Tongan nationality. Birth in Tonga alone to two foreign parents is not a general open jus soli path for ordinary foreign residents.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through a Tongan parent

routes.title: Tongan nationality through a Tongan parent
routes.summary: A child of a Tongan national parent is Tongan under nationality law, subject to registration rules for birth abroad.

pathways.label: Tongan nationality through a Tongan parent

routes.title: Naturalization after five years residence
routes.summary: Foreigners who have resided in Tonga for five years or more may, with the consent of the King, take the oath of allegiance and receive a certificate of naturalization. Language, character, and permanent settlement intent apply under the Nationality Act framework. Grant is highly discretionary.

pathways.label: Naturalization after five years residence

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through a qualifying parent
routes.summary: Citizenship at birth follows the federal parentage rules rather than territorial birth; the law also protects certain children of unknown or stateless parentage.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through a qualifying parent

sources.title: UAE Ministry of Justice — Nationality and Passports Law

routes.title: Emirati nationality through a citizen father
routes.summary: Federal law treats a person born in the UAE or abroad to a qualifying Emirati father as a citizen by operation of law, with additional limited maternal and foundling cases.

pathways.label: Emirati nationality through a citizen father

sources.title: UAE Ministry of Justice — Nationality and Passports Law

routes.title: Exceptional nomination for Emirati nationality
routes.summary: Specified professionals, scientists, inventors, intellectuals and talented people may be nominated for nationality when their category-specific conditions are met.

pathways.label: Exceptional nomination for Emirati nationality
pathways.note: There is no public self-application route or guaranteed timeline.

routes.title: Investor nomination for Emirati nationality
routes.summary: The 2021 exceptional categories allow a property-owning investor to be nominated for nationality by designated UAE authorities.

pathways.label: Investor nomination for Emirati nationality
pathways.note: This is not a purchasable or threshold-based CBI programme. Golden Visa investment residence is separate.

routes.title: Citizenship at birth to prevent statelessness
routes.summary: A child born in Türkiye who cannot acquire any nationality from the parents becomes Turkish from birth; a foundling is presumed born in Türkiye.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth to prevent statelessness

sources.title: Türkiye NVI — Acquisition of Turkish citizenship

routes.title: Turkish citizenship by descent
routes.summary: A child acquires Turkish citizenship through a Turkish mother or father, subject to the parentage rules in Citizenship Law No. 5901.

pathways.label: Turkish citizenship by descent

routes.title: Exceptional citizenship through qualifying investment
routes.summary: Foreign investors meeting a prescribed property, capital, deposit, securities, pension-fund or job-creation condition may acquire citizenship by presidential decision.

pathways.label: Exceptional citizenship through qualifying investment
pathways.note: The property variant is modeled here; official guidance also lists USD 500,000 alternatives and a 50-job option.

sources.title: Invest in Türkiye — Acquiring Property and Citizenship

routes.title: Turkish naturalization after residence
routes.summary: An adult can apply under the general provisions after five continuous years of residence and satisfaction of settlement, language, character, health and security conditions.

pathways.label: Turkish naturalization after residence

sources.title: Türkiye NVI — Acquisition of Turkish citizenship

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through parentage or foundling status
routes.summary: A person is Egyptian at birth when born to an Egyptian father or mother. A person born in Egypt of unknown parents, including a foundling presumed born in Egypt, is also Egyptian. Birth in Egypt alone to identified foreign parents is not general jus soli.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through parentage or foundling status

sources.title: Egypt Law No. 154 of 2004 amending the Nationality Law

routes.title: Egyptian nationality through an Egyptian parent
routes.summary: A person born of an Egyptian father or an Egyptian mother is Egyptian under Law No. 26 of 1975 as equalized by Law No. 154 of 2004.

pathways.label: Egyptian nationality through an Egyptian parent

sources.title: Egypt Law No. 154 of 2004 amending the Nationality Law

routes.title: Egyptian citizenship through investment programmes
routes.summary: The Prime Minister's citizenship unit administers direct routes through a non-refundable contribution, property, an investment project or a central-bank deposit. No multi-year residence period is required before the investment programme grant.

pathways.label: Egyptian citizenship through investment programmes
pathways.note: Official programme pages list four investment options starting at USD 250,000. Approval remains discretionary after security review; temporary residence may be issued only to complete the chosen investment programme.

sources.title: Egyptian Cabinet — Citizenship through Investment Programmes
GAFI — Unit for Granting Egyptian Citizenship in Exchange for Investment

routes.title: Ordinary naturalization after ten consecutive years
routes.summary: Under Article 4 of Law No. 26 of 1975, a foreign adult may seek discretionary Egyptian nationality after ten consecutive years of normal legal residence in Egypt, with Arabic-language, means and character review by the Minister of Interior.

pathways.label: Ordinary naturalization after ten consecutive years
pathways.note: Residence eligibility is not an automatic grant. Shorter statutory routes exist for some Egyptian-origin and other special categories, and investment/property pathways are modeled separately; those are not collapsed into this ordinary record.

routes.title: Citizenship by birth in the UK
routes.summary: For births from 1983, a child is normally automatically British when a parent is British or settled at the time of birth.

pathways.label: British or settled parent

sources.title: GOV.UK — British citizenship for people born in the UK

routes.title: Citizenship through a British parent
routes.summary: British citizenship can usually pass one generation to a child born outside the UK, subject to the parent’s status and the date and place of birth.

pathways.label: British parent
pathways.note: Some categories are automatic and others require registration.

sources.title: GOV.UK — British citizenship through a British parent

routes.title: Naturalization after settlement
routes.summary: The standard route combines five years of residence with at least 12 months holding indefinite leave, settled status, or indefinite leave to enter.

pathways.label: Standard settled route
pathways.note: The residence and settled-status periods overlap when settlement was obtained earlier.

pathways.label: Spouse or civil partner of a British citizen
pathways.note: No additional 12-month wait after settlement applies to this variant.

sources.title: GOV.UK — Naturalization with indefinite leave or settled status

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through a U.S. citizen parent
routes.summary: A child born abroad can acquire citizenship at birth when the citizen parent meets the applicable residence, physical-presence, parentage, and date-of-birth rules.

pathways.label: One U.S. citizen parent for births from 14 November 1986
pathways.note: Different historical and family configurations use different statutory tests.

sources.title: U.S. Department of State — Citizenship at birth abroad

routes.title: Citizenship by birth in the United States
routes.summary: Federal statute grants citizenship at birth to a person born in the United States and subject to its jurisdiction.

pathways.label: Born in the United States and subject to its jurisdiction

routes.title: Naturalization after permanent residence
routes.summary: The standard route requires five years as a lawful permanent resident and at least 30 months of physical presence in that period.

pathways.label: Five-year LPR route
pathways.note: Continuous residence, character, English, civics, and oath requirements also apply.

sources.title: USCIS — Naturalization for lawful permanent residents

routes.title: Uruguayan legal citizenship by habitual residence
routes.summary: A foreign adult of good conduct may seek legal citizenship after three years of habitual residence with family constituted in Uruguay, or five years without, while proving qualifying means or activity.

pathways.label: Uruguayan legal citizenship by habitual residence
pathways.note: Without family constituted in Uruguay the period is five years. An absence exceeding six continuous months resets the residence period.

sources.title: Uruguay Electoral Court — Legal citizenship application

routes.title: Uruguayan nationality by territorial birth
routes.summary: Every person born anywhere in the territory of Uruguay is a natural citizen and Uruguayan national.

pathways.label: Uruguayan nationality by territorial birth

routes.title: Uruguayan nationality through a parent
routes.summary: A child of a Uruguayan mother or father is a Uruguayan national regardless of birthplace; constitutional natural-citizenship exercise involves the applicable connection and registration steps.

pathways.label: Uruguayan nationality through a parent

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through a Samoan parent
routes.summary: Birth to a Samoan parent creates Samoan citizenship. Birth in Samoa alone to two foreign parents is not general unrestricted jus soli.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through a Samoan parent

routes.title: Samoan citizenship through a Samoan parent
routes.summary: A child of a Samoan citizen parent is Samoan under the Citizenship Act lineage rules.

pathways.label: Samoan citizenship through a Samoan parent

routes.title: Citizenship Investment Act exists; current programme operation needs verification
routes.summary: Samoa has a Citizenship Investment Act and was reviewing the programme framework in 2025, but current application operations and qualifying guidelines are not yet verified for live recommendations.

pathways.label: Citizenship Investment Act exists; current programme operation needs verification

routes.title: Naturalization after residence
routes.summary: Foreign adults may apply after five years continuous permanent residence, with character and intention-to-reside conditions. Grant is discretionary.

pathways.label: Naturalization after residence
